archetype_reasoning: The dominant pattern across both resolved and open trades is near-certainty NO positions — buying NO at 0.983-0.999 on long-tail candidates and unlikely outcomes to collect small premiums. The Trump Fed chair nomination cluster (8+ trades all at 0.992-0.999) is a textbook yield-harvesting sweep across mutually exclusive low-probability outcomes. Directionality of 1.0 reflects consistent one-sided positioning, but the positions themselves are not genuine forecasting — they are premium collection on obvious eliminations.
domain_blindspots: Trump_US: near-certainty sweeps inflate accuracy without demonstrating real forecasting — 10/10 wins are largely yield harvests not predictive calls
Other: 3 losses including the Mamdani 0.967 YES near-certainty loss shows vulnerability when betting on near-resolved outcomes that flip
domain_summary: This trader shows broad market coverage (27 distinct markets) but genuine edge is hard to distinguish from yield harvesting. The few contested-price entries (Hungary PM at 0.45, Trump approval at 0.58) are the only signals worth treating as real forecasts.
signal_weight_reasoning: With only 24 resolved trades and a dominant yield-harvesting pattern, the high accuracy figures are structurally inflated by near-certainty NO sweeps. Contested-price trades are rare and the sample is too thin to establish genuine forecasting skill. The 1.0 directionality further undermines signal quality — this trader almost never expresses a YES conviction on contested markets.
behavioural_flags: Systematic near-certainty NO sweeps across mutually exclusive candidate markets (Fed chair nominations, Chilean presidential candidates)
Directionality of 1.0 — no genuine two-sided market engagement
Cluster trading: multiple simultaneous low-odds positions in the same event resolved batch
One notable contested-price YES bet (Mamdani at 0.967) resulted in a loss — suggests occasional overconfidence near resolution
Small position sizing on most open trades (18-50 shares) with one larger outlier (James Solomon 229 shares at 0.80)
risk_patterns: Near-certainty position losses: Mamdani 0.967 YES lost — tail risk on apparent sure-things
Thin sample (24 resolved) means a few adverse outcomes could significantly alter ELO profile
Concentration in yield-harvesting clusters inflates win rate without proportional PnL — realized PnL of only $35 despite strong win rate confirms low expected value per trade
Open positions include some genuinely contested bets (Bolsonaro 0.429, NC Senate NO at 0.75) alongside yield harvests — inconsistent strategy

notable_calls.market: Will Trump's approval rating be 40.0–41.9% on Dec 31?
notable_calls.significance: One of the few genuinely contested-price entries; winning this at 0.58 is the strongest evidence of real forecasting ability in the dataset

notable_calls.market: Will the next PM of Hungary be Péter Magyar?
notable_calls.significance: Contested-price entry that lost — important counterpoint to the inflated accuracy rate; shows real forecasting exposure carries real risk

notable_calls.market: Will Zohran Mamdani get 50-60% of vote in 2025 NYC mayoral election?
notable_calls.significance: Near-certainty YES that lost — demonstrates that tail risks in yield-harvesting positions do materialise

notable_calls.market: Trump Fed chair nomination sweep (8 candidates)
notable_calls.significance: Prototypical yield-harvesting cluster — all won but collectively represent a single structural bet with minimal informational content

open_positions_summary: Current book is split between yield harvests (Trump approval ≥50% NO at 0.996, Israeli PM long-shots at 0.982-0.994, Chilean candidates at 0.91-0.992) and a few genuinely contested calls (James Solomon YES at 0.80, Flávio Bolsonaro YES at 0.429, NC Senate NO at 0.75). The Zelenskyy-Putin meeting positions form a near-complete coverage sweep at various venues. The Bolsonaro and NC Senate bets are the most interesting signal-bearing positions.
watch_items: Monitor James Solomon (Jersey City mayor) position — 229 shares at 0.80 is the largest single contested position and reveals directional conviction
Bolsonaro 2026 YES at 0.429 is a genuine forecasting signal — track whether trader adds to this position
NC Senate NO at 0.75 against Republican favourite — contrarian signal worth monitoring
Watch whether the Zelenskyy-Putin meeting sweep resolves cleanly or produces any losses from the venue-specific NO positions
Track if realized PnL growth remains low relative to win rate — persistent gap would confirm yield-harvesting as dominant strategy
summary: This trader presents strong surface-level statistics (high win rate, diverse market coverage) that are almost entirely attributable to systematic yield-harvesting via near-certainty NO sweeps on long-tail candidates. With only $35 realized PnL across 24 trades, the structural strategy is confirmed as low-margin premium collection rather than genuine forecasting. The three contested-price entries (Trump approval, Magyar PM, Bolsonaro) are the only trades worth treating as real signals, and two of those lost. Copy-following this trader on yield-harvest positions is inadvisable; the Bolsonaro and NC Senate open positions are the only live signals worth monitoring.
